behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is algorithm quality inside the heuristic itself (MOCK-MISS: single logic boundary). No required OS/network/subprocess (REAL-INFRA: none). No AI/ML dependence (LLM-DEP: none). Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely computational — "heuristic... no more than 1.5x... up to 20 stops" — with no external infrastructure or cross-service handoff required.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic coordinate sets (<=20 stops), compute exact optimal length via brute force for small N fixtures, run heuristic, and assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is deterministic formula/mapping logic (distance + configured speed -> time), so Unit catches real failures. No real OS/network/subprocess required (REAL-INFRA: no). No model-output dependence (LLM-DEP: no). Rejected Integration because the behavior text scopes to computation correctness: "computed correctly from the distance... and configured average speed."
behaviors.test_description: Call estimator with fixed segment distances and road-type speed config fixtures; assert per-segment and total time windows match expected arithmetic, including edge cases (rounding, zero distance, mixed road types).

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: This is validator logic on two numeric inputs (estimated time vs remaining shift), with deterministic output (reject + excess). No infrastructure needed (REAL-INFRA: no), no AI/ML (LLM-DEP: no). Rejected higher levels because behavior text is local rule evaluation: "rejects... exceeds... returning the excess amount."
behaviors.test_description: Invoke validator with boundary cases (equal, just over, far over, under) and assert accept/reject flag and exact excess value calculations.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module handoff: geocoding result handling -> filtering -> optimizer input -> skipped-address reporting (MOCK-MISS: cross-module state propagation). Integration with stubbed geocoder is sufficient. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; the behavior already specifies the API outcome "returns no results" and can be reproduced by in-process fake. (b) no AI/ML output dependence. (c) no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service with an in-process fake geocoding client returning coordinates for valid addresses and no-results for one address. Assert output route excludes failed address, optimization still runs on remaining stops, and response contains the exact skipped address list.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration across dedup/aggregation and routing: duplicates must merge payloads and route must contain one visit. Unit on only dedupe logic could miss handoff errors into optimizer (MOCK-MISS: yes). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no AI/ML dependence; no multi-agentic sequence. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is deterministic service dataflow: "consolidated... combined delivery items, not visited twice."
behaviors.test_description: Run service pipeline with duplicate-address inputs carrying distinct items, then assert output has one stop for that address, combined item set/count is correct, and route sequence includes that stop exactly once.
